title: iberis intermedia subsp. collina
intro: Iberis intermedia subsp. collina, also known as the Rock Candy Flower or Alpine Candytuft, is a flowering plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It is a low-growing perennial with delicate white or pink flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Iberis intermedia subsp. collina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to ease respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
